IM DLM3054-17EN - Chapter 5 Commands

Chapter 5

    5.1             List of Commands


  Command                         Function                                                                     Page

  ACQuire Group
  :ACQuire?                       Queries all waveform acquisition settings.                                   5-60
  :ACQuire
    :AVERage?                     Queries all settings related to averaging and waveform acquisition counts.   5-60

    :AVERage
       :COUNt                     Sets or queries the attenuation constant of exponential averaging or the     5-60
                                  average count of simple averaging when in averaging mode.
      :COUNt                      Sets or queries the number of waveform acquisitions to perform in normal,    5-60
                                  envelope, and averaging modes.
      :MODE                       Sets or queries the waveform acquisition mode.                               5-60
      :RESolution                 Sets or queries the high resolution mode on/off state.                       5-60
      :RLENgth                    Sets or queries the record length.                                           5-60
      :SAMPling                   Sets or queries the sampling mode.                                           5-60


  ANALysis Group
  :ANALysis?                      Queries all analysis feature settings.                                       5-61
  :ANALysis
    :AHIStogram<x>?               Queries all settings related to the histogram of the analysis feature.       5-61
    :AHIStogram<x>
       :DISPlay                   Sets or queries the waveform histogram display on/off state.                 5-61
       :HORizontal                Sets or queries the horizontal range of the waveform histogram.              5-61
       :MEASure?                  Queries all settings related to the waveform histogram measurement           5-61
                                  (including the mode on/off state).
         :MEASure
           :MODE                  Sets or queries the waveform histogram measurement mode.                     5-61
           :PARameter?            Queries all settings related to the waveform histogram measurement when      5-61
                                  the mode is set to Param.
            :PARameter
              :ALL                Collectively turns on or off all the measurement items of the waveform       5-61
                                  histogram.
                  :<Parameter>?   Queries all settings related to the specified measurement item of the        5-61
                                  waveform histogram.
                  :<Parameter>
                    :STATe        Sets or queries the on/off state of the specified measurement item of the     5-62
                                  waveform histogram.
                    :VALue?       Queries the automatically measured value of the specified measurement         5-62
                                  item of the waveform histogram.
                  :HPOSition<y>   Sets or queries the horizontal position of Cursor1 or Cursor2 of the waveform 5-62
                                  histogram.
                  :VPOSition<y>   Sets or queries the vertical position of Cursor1 or Cursor2 of the waveform 5-62
                                  histogram.
        :MODE                     Sets or queries the target axis of the waveform histogram.                    5-62
        :RANGe                    Sets or queries the measurement source window of the waveform histogram. 5-62
        :TRACe                    Sets or queries the source waveform of the waveform histogram.                5-62
        :VERTical                 Sets or queries the vertical range of the waveform histogram.                 5-63
      :PANalyze?                  Queries all power supply analysis settings.                                   5-63
      :PANalyze
        :HARMonics?               Queries all harmonic analysis settings.                                      5-63
        :HARMonics
           :CCLass?               Queries all settings related to harmonic analysis class C.                   5-63
           :CCLass
             :GETLambda           Sets the current power factor of harmonic analysis class C.                 5-63
             :LAMBda              Sets or queries the power factor of harmonic analysis class C.              5-63
             :MAXCurrent          Sets or queries the fundamental current value of harmonic analysis class C. 5-63
             :OPOWer              Sets or queries whether active power 25 W of harmonic analysis class C will 5-63
                                  be exceeded.
            :CLASs                Sets or queries the applicable class of the DUT in harmonic analysis.       5-63

5.1 List of
  Command                                Function                                                                          Page
        :DCLass?                         Queries all settings related to harmonic analysis class D.                        5-63
        :DCLass
          :POWer                         Sets or queries the power value of harmonic analysis class D.                     5-64
        :DMODe                           Sets or queries the display mode of harmonic analysis.                            5-64
        :DETail?                         Queries all settings related to the harmonic analysis result list.                5-64
        :DETail
          :DISPlay                       Sets or queries the display mode of the harmonic analysis result list.            5-64
          :LIST
             :ITEM?                      Queries all items that will be displayed in the harmonic analysis result list.    5-64
             :VALue?                     Queries all the data for the specified analysis number in the harmonic            5-64
                                         analysis result list.
         :GROuping                       Sets or queries the harmonic analysis grouping.                                   5-64
         :IECEdition                     Sets or queries measurement standard (IEC61000-4-7) of the harmonic               5-64
                                         analysis measurement mode.
        :RMS                             Queries the rms value harmonic analysis.                                          5-65
        :SPOint                          Sets or queries the computation start point of harmonic analysis.                 5-65
        :SVOLtage                        Sets or queries the supply voltage of harmonic analysis.                          5-65
        :THD                             Queries the harmonic distortion factor of harmonic computation.                   5-65
      :I2T?                              Queries all Joule integral settings.                                              5-65
      :I2T
        :MATH                            Sets or queries the Joule integral waveform display on/off state.                 5-65
        :MEASure?                        Queries all settings related to the automated measurement of the Joule            5-65
                                         integral.
        :MEASure
           :I2T?                   Queries Joule integral settings.                                                 5-65
           :I2T
              :COUNt?              Queries the continuous statistical processing count of the Joule integral.       5-65
              :{MAXimum|MEAN|MINim Queries the Joule integral statistics.                                           5-65
              um|SDEViation}?
              :STATe               Sets or queries whether the Joule integral is to be measured.                    5-65
              :VALue?              Queries automatically measured value of the Joule integral.                      5-66
        :RANGe                     Sets or queries the measurement source window.                                   5-66
        :SCALe?                    Queries all scaling settings.                                                    5-66
        :SCALe
           :CENTer                 Sets or queries the center value for manual scaling.                             5-66
           :MODE                   Sets or queries the scale mode.                                                  5-66
           :SENSitivity            Sets or queries the sensitivity for manual scaling.                              5-66
        :TRANge                    Sets or queries the measurement time period.                                     5-66
      :SETup?                      Queries all power supply analysis input settings.                                5-67
      :SETup
        :ADESkew                   Executes auto deskewing for power supply analysis.                               5-67
        :I?                        Queries all current input channel settings for power supply analysis.            5-67
        :I
           :DESKew                 Sets or queries auto deskewing of the current input channel for power supply 5-67
                                   analysis.
           :INPut                  Sets or queries the current input channels for power supply analysis.            5-67
           :PROBe                  Queries all current-to-voltage conversion ratio settings of the probe            5-67
                                   connected to the current input channel for power supply analysis.
        :U?                        Queries all voltage input channel settings for power supply analysis.            5-67
        :U
           :DESKew                 Sets or queries auto deskewing of the voltage input channel for power supply 5-67
                                   analysis.
           :INPut                  Sets or queries the voltage input channels for power supply analysis.            5-68
           :PROBe                  Sets or queries the probe attenuation of the voltage input channels for power 5-68
                                   supply analysis.
        :RTRace                    Sets or queries the deskewing source trace for power supply analysis.            5-68
      :SOA?                        Queries all settings related to the XY display for safe operating area analysis. 5-68
      :SOA
        :CURSor?                   Queries all settings related to the cursor measurement on the XY display for 5-68
                                   safe operating area analysis.
        :CURSor
           :X<x>?                  Queries all settings related to the horizontal cursors on the XY display for     5-68
                                   safe operating area analysis.

                                            safe operating area analysis.

         :SWLoss?                           Queries all switching loss settings.                                           5-69
         :SWLoss
           :CCALc                           Sets or queries the total loss equation.                                          5-69


           :DPROximal?                      Queries all distal, mesial, and proximal settings.                                5-69
           :DPROximal
              :MODE                         Sets or queries the distal, mesial, and proximal point mode setting.              5-70

              :PERCent                      Sets or queries the distal, mesial, and proximal points as percentages.           5-70
              :UNIT                         Sets or queries the distal, mesial, and proximal points as voltages.              5-70
           :ILEVel                          Sets or queries the current level for determining the loss zero period of total   5-70
                                            loss.
            :INDicator                      Sets or queries the total loss measurement location indicator.                    5-70
            :MATH                           Sets or queries the power waveform display on/off state.                          5-70
            :MEASure?                       Queries all settings related to the automated measurement of power supply         5-70
                                            parameters.
            :MEASure

              :<Parameter>?                 Queries the setting of a power supply analysis parameter.                         5-70
              :<Parameter>
                 :COUNt?              Queries the continuous statistical processing count of the power supply                 5-71
                                      analysis parameter.
                 :{MAXimum|MEAN|MINim Queries a statistical value of a power supply analysis parameter.                       5-71
                 um|SDEViation}?
                 :STATe               Sets or queries the on/off state of the power supply analysis parameter.                5-71
                 :VALue?              Queries the automatically measured value of a power supply parameter.                   5-71
              :CONTinuous?            Queries all settings related to the normal statistical processing of                    5-71
                                      automatically measured total loss.
              :CONTinuous
                 :RESTart             Restarts the normal statistical processing of automatically measured total              5-71
                                      loss.
                 :TLCHange            Sets or queries whether the normal statistical processing of automatically              5-72
                                      measured total loss is to be restarted.
              :CYCLe
                 :ABORt               Aborts the execution of the cyclic statistical processing of automatically              5-72
                                      measured total loss.
                 :EXECute             Executes the cyclic statistical processing of automatically measured total              5-72
                                      loss.
              :HISTory
                 :ABORt               Aborts the execution of the statistical processing of the history waveform of           5-72
                                      automatically measured total loss.
                 :EXECute             Executes the statistical processing of the history waveform of automatically            5-72
                                      measured total loss.
              :MODE                   Sets or queries the statistical processing type of automatically measured               5-72
                                      total loss.
              :ZLINkage               Sets or queries the linkage (on/off) between the result numbers and zoom                5-72
                                      positions of the results of automatically measured total loss.
            :METhod                   Sets or queries the calculation method for high and low points.                         5-72
            :RANGe                    Sets or queries the measurement source window.                                          5-72

  ASETup Group
  :ASETup
    :EXECute                              Executes auto setup.                                                           5-81
    :UNDO                                 Undoes auto setup.                                                             5-81


  CALibrate Group
  :CALibrate?                             Queries all calibration settings.                                              5-82
  :CALibrate
    [:EXECute]                            Executes calibration.                                                          5-82
    :MODE                                 Sets or queries the auto calibration on/off state.                             5-82


  CHANnel Group
  :CHANnel<x>?                            Queries all vertical axis settings of a channel.                               5-83

    :ASCale
       [:EXECute]                         Executes auto scaling of a channel.                                            5-83
    :BWIDth                               Sets or queries the input filter of the specified channel.                     5-83
    :COUPling                             Sets or queries the input coupling of the specified channel.                   5-83
    :DESKew                               Sets or queries the deskewing of the specified channel.                        5-83
    :DISPlay                              Sets or queries the display on/off state of the specified channel.             5-83

                                          display) .
      :LABel?                             Queries all settings related to the waveform label of the specified channel.   5-83
      :LABel
        :DEFine                           Sets or queries the waveform label of a channel.                               5-83


5.1 List of
  Command              Function                                                                        Page
      :DISPlay         Sets or queries the display on/off state of the specified channel’s waveform    5-84
                       label.
      :LSCale?         Queries all the linear scaling settings of the specified channel.               5-84
      :LSCale
        :AVALue        Sets or queries the scaling coefficient A.                                      5-84
        :BVALue        Sets or queries offset B.                                                       5-84
        :MODE          Sets or queries the on/off state of linear scaling.                             5-84
        :UNIT          Sets or queries a unit that is attached to linear scaling results.              5-84
      :OFFSet          Sets or queries the offset voltage of the specified channel.                    5-84
      :POSition        Sets or queries the vertical position of the specified channel.                 5-84
      :PROBe?          Queries all settings related to the probe attenuation of the specified channel. 5-84
      :PROBe
        :DZCalibrate   Executes demagnetization and zero calibration of the specified channel.         5-84
        [:MODE]        Sets or queries the probe attenuation of the specified channel.                 5-85
        :PZCalibrate   Executes zero calibration of the specified channel’s current probe.             5-85
      :VARiable        Sets or queries the specified channel’s voltage scale in 0.01 V/div steps.      5-85
      :VDIV            Sets or queries the specified channel’s voltage scale (V/div).                  5-85


  CLEar Group
  :CLEar               Clears traces.                                                                  5-86


  COMMunicate Group
  :COMMunicate?        Queries all communication settings.                                             5-87
  :COMMunicate
    :HEADer            Sets or queries whether a header is added to the response to a query      5-87
                       (example with header: “CHANNEL1:PROBE:MODE 10”; example without
                       header: “10”).
      :LOCKout         Sets/clears local lockout.                                                5-87
      :OPSE            Sets or queries the overlap command that is used by the *OPC, *OPC?, and 5-87
                       *WAI commands.
      :OPSR?           Queries the operation pending status register.                            5-87
      :OVERlap         Sets or queries the commands that operate as overlap commands.            5-87
      :REMote          Sets the instrument to remote or local mode. On is remote mode.           5-87
      :VERBose         Sets or queries whether the response to a query is returned fully spelled 5-88
                       out (e.g., CHANNEL1:PROBE:MODE 10) or in its abbreviated form (e.g.,
                       CHAN:PROB 10).
      :WAIT            Waits for a specified extended event to occur.                            5-88
      :WAIT?           Creates the response that is returned when a specified extended event     5-88
                       occurs.

           :LEVel                  Sets or queries the trigger level of the specified trigger source for enhanced     5-324
                                   triggering.
           ULLevel                 Sets or queries the upper and lower limits of the enhanced trigger source          5-325
                                   level.
         :TIME<y>                  Sets or queries the time for enhanced triggering.                                  5-325

  Common Command Group
  *CAL                          Executes calibration and queries the result.                                    5-338
  *CLS

                                Clears the standard event register, extended event register, and error queue. 5-338
  *ESE                          Sets or queries the standard event enable register.                             5-338
  *ESR?                         Queries and clears the standard event register.                                 5-338
  *IDN?                         Queries the instrument model.                                                   5-338
  *OPC                          Sets bit 0 (the OPC bit) of the standard event register to 1 upon the           5-338
                                completion of the specified overlap command.
  *OPC?                         When you send *OPC?, the instrument returns ASCII code 1 when the               5-339
                                specified overlap command is completed.
  *OPT?                         Queries the installed options.                                                  5-339
  *RST                          Initializes the settings.                                                       5-339
  *SRE                          Sets or queries the service request enable register value.                      5-339
  *STB?                         Queries the status byte register value.                                         5-339
  *TST?                         Executes a self-test and queries the result. The self-test consists of tests of 5-340
                                each kind of internal memory.
  *WAI                          Holds the execution of the subsequent command until the specified overlap 5-340
                                command is completed.

    5.3           ANALysis Group

The power supply analysis feature (:ANALysis:PANalyze and :ANALysis:PMEAsure commands) is an option
available only for 4-channel models. Power supply analysis commands cannot be used on 2-channel models or
4-channel models without the power supply analysis option.

                                                                  Example    :CHANNEL1:LABEL:DEFINE "CH1"

                                                                  Description You can only use the characters and symbols on
                                                                              the keyboard that appears on the instrument’s
                                                                              screen.

            <x> = 1 to 4 (1 or 2 on 2-channel models)

5.8 COMMunicate Group

:COMMunicate:VERBose
Function    Sets or queries whether the response to
            a query is returned fully spelled out (e.g.,
            CHANNEL1:PROBE:MODE 10) or in its
            abbreviated form (e.g., CHAN:PROB 10).
Syntax      :COMMunicate:VERBose {<Boolean>}
            :COMMunicate:VERBose?

            :COMMUNICATE:VERBOSE?
            -> :COMMUNICATE:VERBOSE


:COMMunicate:WAIT
Function    Waits for a specified extended event to occur.
Syntax      :COMMunicate:WAIT <Register>
            <Register> = 0 to 65535 (Extended event
            register. For more information, see page 6-5.)
Example     :COMMUNICATE:WAIT 65535
Description For information about how to synchronize a
            program using :COMMunicate:WAIT, see page
            4-9.


:COMMunicate:WAIT?
Function    Creates the response that is returned when a
            specified extended event occurs.
Syntax      :COMMunicate:WAIT? <Register>
            <Register> = 0 to 65535 (Extended event
            register. For more information, see page 6-5.)
Example     :COMMUNICATE:WAIT? 65535
            ->
Description Operation pending status register and overlap
            enable register

  When bit 5 (PRN) = 1:
		 Built-in printer operation not complete
  When bit 6 (ACS) = 1:
		 Media access not complete
  When bit 8 (HST) = 1:
		 History search execution not complete
  When bit 11 (SCH) = 1:
		 Edge/pattern search execution not complete

           exponential averaging of FFT analysis.             Syntax     :FFT<x>:DATA:LENGth?

Function   Queries all the information related to the FFT                -> :FFT1:DATA:SEND #6
           waveform data that will be sent.                               (6-digit number of bytes)(data sequence) or
Syntax     :FFT<x>:DATA?                                                  <NRf>,<NRf>,...
           <x> = 1 or 2 (1 only for 2-channel models)         Description The output format of ":FFT<x>:DATA:SEND?"
                                                                         varies depending on the
:FFT<x>:DATA:BYTeorder                                                   ":FFT<x>:DATA:FORMat" setting.
Function   Sets or queries the transmission byte order when              When set to "ASCii," the information is returned
           the FFT waveform data format is binary.                       in this form: <NRf>,<NRf>,...<NRf>
Syntax     :FFT<x>:DATA:BYTeorder {LSBFirst|                             When set to "BINARY," the information is returned
           MSBFirst}                                                     in <block data> format. The data of each point

   5.18 LOGic Group
The commands in this group cannot be used on models without logic input.

   5.19 MATH Group
User-defined computation is an option available only for 4-channel models. User-defined computation commands
cannot be used on 2-channel models or 4-channel models without the user-defined computation option.

Example     The following is an example of the average value   Example     The following is an example of the average value


                                                                           of CH1.
            of CH1.
                                                                           :MEASURE:CHANNEL1:AVERAGE:VALUE?
            :MEASURE:CHANNEL1:AVERAGE:STATE ON
            :MEASURE:CHANNEL1:AVERAGE:STATE?
                                                                           -> :MEASURE:CHANNEL1:AVERAGE:
                                                                           VALUE 10.0000E+03
            -> :MEASURE:CHANNEL1:AVERAGE:STATE 1               Description • If the value is immeasurable, the instrument
                                                                              returns “NAN” (not a number).
                                                                           • The <NRf> is used to specify which iteration
                                                                              of automated measurement to query the
                                                                              measured value from.
                                                                           • If <NRf> is set to 1, the oldest measured value
                                                                              in the automated measurement memory is
                                                                              queried.
                                                                           • If a measured value does not exist at the
                                                                              specified iteration, the instrument returns “NAN”
                                                                              (not a number).
                                                                           • If <NRf> is omitted, the most recent measured
                                                                              value is queried.
                                                                           • If cyclic statistical processing for automated
                                                                              measurement is being executed and <NRf> is
                                                                              specified, the measured values over a cycle in
                                                                              iteration <NRf> from the left of the display on
                                                                              the displayed waveform is queried. If <NRf>
                                                                              is omitted, the measured values over the last
                                                                              cycle on the displayed waveform is queried.
                                                                           • The optional “STATus” at the end is valid when
                                                                              the parameter is set to FALL, NWIDth, PERiod,
                                                                              PWIDth, or RISE. If “STATus” is omitted for
                                                                              these parameters, if the measurement value
                                                                              is less than the measurement resolution, a
                                                                              negative value is returned. If “STATus” in
                                                                              included, “LOW_RESOL” is returned.

                                                               :MEASure:{CHANnel<x>|MATH<x>}:COPY
                                                               Function    Copies the on/off state of all measurement items
                                                                           of a waveform to all other waveforms.
                                                               Syntax      :MEASure:{CHANnel<x>|MATH<x>}:COPY
                                                                           <x> of CHANnel<x> = 1 to 4 (1 or 2 on 2-channel
                                                                           models)
                                                                           <x> of MATH<x> = 1 to 4 (1 or 2 on 2-channel
                                                                           models)
                                                               Example     :MEASURE:CHANNEL1:COPY

:MEASure:USER<x>:VALue?
Function    Queries the automatically measured value of a
            Calc item.
Syntax      :MEASure:USER<x>:VALue? [<NRf>]
            <x> = 1 to
            <NRf> = See the Features Guide for this
            information.
Example     :MEASURE:USER1:VALUE?
            -> :MEASURE:USER1:VALUE 1.000E+00
Description • If the value is immeasurable, the instrument
              returns “NAN” (not a number).
            • The <NRf> is used to specify which iteration
              of automated measurement to query the
              measured value from.
            • If <NRf> is set to 1, the oldest measured value
              in the automated measurement memory is
              queried.
            • If a measured value does not exist at the
              specified iteration, the instrument returns “NAN”
              (not a number).
            • If <NRf> is omitted, the most recent measured
              value is queried.

            <x> = 1 to

Description If you specify an area that does not contain setup
            data, an error occurs.

5-152                                                                                                            IM DLM3054-17EN
    5.23 SEARch Group

The following table indicates how the communication commands for search methods correspond to the search types
that appear when you press a panel key.
  Search Type                                   Communication Command     Setup Menu
                                                                          Panel Key          Search Type
  Edge                                     EDGE                           SEARCH Key         Edge
  Combination of multiple inputs (pattern) PATTern                                           Pattern
  Pulse width                              PULSe                                             Pulse Width
  Timeout period                           TIMeout                                           Timeout

    5.24 SERialbus Group

The following table indicates how the communication commands for serial bus types correspond to the serial bus
types that appear when you press a panel key.
  Serial Bus Type              Communication           Setup Menu
                               Command                 Panel Key                       Serial Bus Type
  CAN                          CAN*                    SHIFT+SEARCH (SERIAL BUS)       CAN
  CAN FD                       CANFD*                  Key                             CAN FD
  CXPI                         CXPI*                                                   CXPI
  FlexRay                      FLEXray*                                                FlexRay
  I2C                          I2C*                                                    I2C
  LIN                          LIN*
                               PSI5*
                                                                                       LIN
  PSI5                                                                                 PSI5 Airbag
  SENT                         SENT*                                                   SENT
  User-defined                 SPATtern                                                User Define
  SPI                          SPI*                                                    SPI
  UART                         UART*                                                   UART
   * CAN, CANFD, CXPI, FLEXray, I2C, LIN, PSI5, SENT, SPI, and UART are serial bus options available only to


     4-channel models. These commands cannot be used on 2-channel models or 4-channel models without these
     options.
:SERialbus<x>?                                                      :SERialbus<x>:ASETup:SWAVeform
Function    Queries all serial bus signal analysis and search       Function    Sets or queries the waveform on which to perform
            settings.                                                           serial bus signal auto setup.

            <x> = 1 to 4 (1 on 2-channel models)                                UPDate}

:SERialbus<x>:ASETup?                                                           <x> = 1 to

            <x> = 1 to 4                                            Description • This command applies to SERialbus1 to

            <x> = 1 to 4                                                          performed on new waveforms that will be

Syntax      :SERialbus<x>:CXPI:SEARch:EXECute
            <x> = 1 to
Example     :SERIALBUS1:CXPI:SEARCH:EXECUTE

5-242                          IM DLM3054-17EN
    5.26 SSTart Group


:SSTart?
Function    Sets the trigger mode to single, starts waveform
            acquisition. The instrument returns 0 if the
            waveform acquisition is stopped within the
            specified timeout. Otherwise, the instrument
            returns 1.
Syntax      :SSTart? {<NRf>}
            <NRf> = 1 to 36000 (timeout value in 100 ms
            steps; start and wait)
            <NRf> = 0 (start only, no timeout)
            <NRf> = –36000 to 1 (timeout value in 100 ms
            steps; wait without starting)
Example     :SSTART?
            -> :SSTART
Description • If the specified timeout is a positive value, the


              instrument starts waveform acquisition in single
              trigger mode and waits for the acquisition to be
              stopped within the specified timeout period.
            • If the specified timeout is 0, the instrument
              starts waveform acquisition and returns 0
              without waiting.
            • If the specified timeout is a negative value, the
              instrument does not start waveform acquisition
              and simply waits for the acquisition to be
              stopped within the specified timeout period.


   5.27 STARt Group

:STARt
Function    Starts waveform acquisition.
Syntax      :STARt
Example     :START
Description Stop the waveform acquisition using “STOP”.

    5.28 STATus Group

The commands in this group are used to make settings and queries related to the communication status feature. There are no front
panel keys that correspond to the commands in this group. For information about status reports, see chapter 6.

    5.30 STORe Group


:STORe?
Function    Queries all the information related to setup data
            in the internal memory.
Syntax      :STORe?


:STORe:SETup<x>?
Function    Queries information about the setup data in the
            specified location of the internal memory.
Syntax      :STORe:SETup<x>?

            <x> = 1 to


:STORe:SETup<x>:COMMent
Function    Sets or queries the comment for the setup data

            that is stored to the specified location in the
            internal memory.


Syntax      :STORe:SETup<x>:COMMent {<String>}
            :STORe:SETup<x>:COMMent?

            <x> = 1 to
            <String> = Up to 16 characters
Example     :STORE:SETUP1:COMMENT "WAVE1"
            :STORE:SETUP1:COMMENT?

            -> :STORE:SETUP1:COMMENT "WAVE1"


:STORe:SETup<x>:DATE?
Function    Queries the date and time of the setup data that

            is stored to the specified location in the internal
            memory.
Syntax      :STORe:SETup<x>:DATE?
            <x> = 1 to
Example     :STORE:SETUP1:DATE?
            -> :STORE:SETUP1:DATE "2018/11/11 10
            :56:22"


:STORe:SETup<x>:EXECute
Function    Saves setup data to the specified location in the
            internal memory.
Syntax      :STORe:SETup<x>:EXECute
            <x> = 1 to
Example     :STORE:SETUP1:EXECUTE


:STORe:SETup<x>:LOCK
Function    Sets or queries the protection on/off state for the
            setup data stored to the specified location in the
            internal memory.
Syntax      :STORe:SETup<x>:LOCK {<Boolean>}
            :STORe:SETup<x>:LOCK?
            <x> = 1 to
Example     :STORE:SETUP1:LOCK ON
            :STORE:SETUP1:LOCK?
            -> :STORE:SETUP1:LOCK

   5.33 TRIGger Group
The following table indicates how the communication commands for trigger types correspond to the trigger types that
appear when you press a panel key.
  Trigger Type                               Communication   Setup Menu
                                             Command         Panel Key                              Trigger Type
  Edge                                       SIMPle          EDGE key                               —
  OR of multiple edge triggers (edge OR)     OR              ENHANCED key                           Edge OR
  Combination of multiple inputs (pattern)   PATTern                                                Pattern
  Pulse width                                PULSe                                                  Pulse Width
  Rise/fall time                             RFTime                                                 Rise/Fall Time
  Runt                                       RUNT                                                   Runt
  Timeout period                             TIMeout                                                Timeout
  Window                                     WINDow                                                 Window
  Window OR                                  WOR                                                    Window OR
  Edge interval trigger                      INTerval                                               Interval
  Serial trigger                             CAN*                                                   CAN
                                             CANFD*                                                 CAN FD
                                             CXPI*                                                  CXPI
                                             FLEXray*                                               FlexRay
                                             I2C*                                                   I2C
                                             LIN*                                                   LIN
                                             PSI5*                                                  PSI5 Airbag
                                             SENT*                                                  SENT
                                             SPATtern                                               User Define
                                             SPI*                                                   SPI
                                             UART*                                                  UART
  TV                                         TV:HDTV                                                HDTV
                                             TV:NTSC                                                NTSC
                                             TV:PAL                                                 PAL
                                             TV:SDTV                                                SDTV(480/60p)
                                             TV:USERdefine                                          UserDef TV
  Combination                                COMBination     B TRIG key                             —
  Forced                                     FORCe           SHIFT+B TRIG (FORCE TRIG) key          —

  * CAN, CANFD, CXPI, FLEXray, I2C, LIN, PSI5, SENT, SPI, and UART are serial bus options available only to
    4-channel models. These commands cannot be used on 2-channel models or 4-channel models without these
    options.

   5.34 WAVeform Group
The commands in this group deal with the acquired waveform data. There are no front panel keys that correspond to
the commands in this group.

:WAVeform?                                                  :WAVeform:ALL:SEND?
Function   Queries all the waveform data information.       Function    Queries the waveform data specified by
Syntax     :WAVeform?                                                   :WAVeform:ALL:TRACe.
                                                            Syntax      :WAVeform:ALL:SEND? {<NRf>}
:WAVeform:ALL?                                                          <NRf>= 0 to the acquisition count
                                                            Example     :WAVEFORM:ALL:SEND? 0
Function   Queries all settings related to the
                                                                        -> #800007558
           :WAVeform:ALL:SEND? query.
                                                                        03 00 85 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
Syntax     :WAVeform:ALL?
                                                                        00 00 E2 04
                                                                        00 00 00 00 00 00 E2 04 00 00 62 00
                                                                        AC 11 44 00
                                                                        6C 00 26 00 08 00 3C 18 3E 18 4E 18
                                                                        0A 18 18 18 ...
                                                            Description • You can query waveform data during
                                                                          measurement.
                                                                        • You cannot query waveform data when the
                                                                          trigger mode is set to Single or NSingle.
                                                                        • You cannot query waveform data when the
                                                                          display is in roll mode.
                                                                        • You cannot query waveform data when
                                                                          continuous statistical processing of
                                                                          automatically measured waveform parameters
                                                                          or user-defined computation is enabled.
                                                                        • The data format is shown below.
                                                            		 #8 (eight digits for the number of transmission
                                                                          data bytes in decimal format) <- Block data
                                                                          header
                                                            		 (two bytes for the number of traces)(eight
                                                                          bytes for the acquisition count) <- Common
                                                                          information
                                                            		 (four bytes for the trace number)(eight bytes for
                                                                          the reserved area)(four bytes for the number of
                                                                          data points) <- Trace information
                                                            		 (data sequence) . . .
                                                            		 (four bytes for the trace number)(eight bytes for
                                                                          the reserved area)(four bytes for the number of
                                                                          data points) <- Trace information
                                                            		 (data sequence) . . .
                                                            		 . . .                             (repeated for all the traces)
                                                                        • The common information and trace information
                                                                          is written in little-endian byte order.
                                                                        • The data sequence format is WORD. The byte
                                                                          order is little endian.
                                                                        • You can query up to 12.5 kPoints in a record.
                                                                        • <NRf> You can acquire the latest waveform
                                                                          data by setting (the acquisition count) to 0.
                                                                        • To acquire waveforms so that the same
                                                                          waveform data is not acquired, specify the
                                                                          acquisition count of the common information
                                                                          acquired with this command the last time using
                                                                          <NRf>. If the specified acquisition count and
                                                                          the DLM200's acquisition count are the same
                                                                          at the point in time that the query is received,
                                                                          #800000000 is returned.


5-326                                                                                                         IM DLM3054-17EN

5.35 WPARameter Group

:WPARameter<x>:VTDisplay
Function    Sets or queries the VT waveform display on/off
            state.
Syntax      :WPARameter<x>:VTDisplay {<Boolean>}
            :WPARameter<x>:VTDisplay?
            <x> = 1 or 2 (1 only for 2-channel models)
Example     :WPARAMETER1:VTDISPLAY ON
            :WPARAMETER1:VTDISPLAY?
            -> :WPARAMETER1:VTDISPLAY


:WPARameter<x>:WAIT?
Function    Waits for the completion of automated
            measurement with a timeout.
Syntax      :WPARameter<x>:WAIT? {<NRf>}
            <x> = 1 or 2 (1 only for 2-channel models)
            <NRf> = 1 to 36000 (timeout period, 10 ms)
Example     :WPARAMETER1:WAIT?
            -> :WPARAMETER1:WAIT
Description If the execution of automated measurement is
            completed within the timeout period, 0 is returned.
            If it is not completed or if automated measurement
            is not being performed, 1 is returned. Even if you
            set a long timeout period, 0 is returned as soon
            as the automated measurement is completed.

    5.38 Common Command Group
The commands in this group are defined in USBTMC-USB488 and are independent from the instrument-specific
functions. There are no front panel keys that correspond to the commands in this group.

*CAL? (CALibrate)                                                  *ESR? (standard Event Status

5.38 Common Command Group

*TST?
Function    Executes a self-test and queries the result. The
            self-test consists of tests of each kind of internal
            memory.
Syntax      *TST?
Example     *TST?
            -> 0
Description This command returns 0 if the self-test is
            successful and 1 if it is not.


*WAI (WAIt)
Function    Holds the execution of the subsequent command
            until the specified overlap command is completed.
Syntax      *WAI
Example     *WAI
Description • For information about how to synchronize a
              program using *WAI, see page 4-8.
            • The :COMMunicate:OPSE command is used
              to specify the overlap command.
